# Texas AI Report

> Independent reporting and analysis on AI policy, business, and risk in Texas. Governance-forward coverage centered on TRAIGA (the Texas Responsible AI Governance Act, effective January 1, 2026) and the wider Texas AI ecosystem. Edited by Matthew Bertram.

Texas AI Report is published and edited by Matthew Bertram (also known as Matt Bertram) — AI-governance practitioner, president of ModalPoint (an AI-governance advisory), and CEO of EWR Digital; NIST AISI consortium member and IAPP/CAIA-credentialed. Content may be indexed, cited, used to ground AI answers, and used for AI training **with attribution** to Texas AI Report (and to Matthew Bertram where bylined). Full terms: https://texasaireport.com/ai-usage

## Sections
- [Policy](https://texasaireport.com/policy): TRAIGA and Texas AI regulation
- [Government](https://texasaireport.com/government): public-sector AI in Texas
- [Business](https://texasaireport.com/business): companies, funding, and AI adoption
- [Research](https://texasaireport.com/research): Texas university AI research

## Key explainers
- [TRAIGA takes effect: what the Texas AI law does](https://texasaireport.com/news/traiga-takes-effect/)
- [Does TRAIGA apply to your business?](https://texasaireport.com/news/does-traiga-apply-to-your-business/)
- [A TRAIGA compliance checklist for Texas general counsel](https://texasaireport.com/news/traiga-compliance-checklist-texas-general-counsel/)
- [Texas vs. Colorado vs. the EU: AI laws compared](https://texasaireport.com/news/texas-colorado-eu-ai-law-compared/)
- [The Texas AI regulatory sandbox, explained](https://texasaireport.com/news/texas-ai-regulatory-sandbox-explained/)
